message:
fix(cli): refuse to kill non-Agent-Mail processes when clearing port

Previously, auto_clear_port would kill any process holding the
configured port, including unrelated services that happened to be
listening on the same port. This was dangerous — a user running another
HTTP server on the default port would have it silently terminated.

Changes:
- auto_clear_port now returns CliError instead of silently proceeding
- When the port is held by an Agent Mail server, it is stopped as before
- When the port is held by a non-Agent-Mail process, return an error
  asking the user to stop it manually or choose a different port
- kill_port_holder now verifies the port is actually free after the
  kill attempt and returns an error if it failed
- Propagate errors from auto_clear_port via ? in handle_default_launch
  and handle_serve_http so the user sees a clear diagnostic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
